prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) in PySCF, **gas phase** (no solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals, chemkit default). The finite-difference Fukui functions were built from Mulliken (PySCF) partial charges of the neutral (N electrons) plus the cation (N−1) and anion (N+1) states; the derived ion states were cation charge +1 / multiplicity 2 (doublet) and anion charge −1 / multiplicity 2 (doublet).

Geometry provenance: coordinates taken as-is from the user-supplied file `aniline.xyz` (14 atoms: N, 6×C, 7×H); no geometry optimization was performed in this task (single-point energies at the supplied geometry).

**Headline result — most electrophilic site (largest f+):** atom C3 (1-based label 3; 0-based index 2), **f+ = 2.2975867** (dimensionless Fukui function for nucleophilic attack). By symmetry the equivalent ortho carbon C4 has f+ = 2.2965637. The **most nucleophilic site (largest f−)** is the amine N (atom 1), f− = 0.20452359. The dual descriptor is largest and positive at C3 (2.2310153), consistent with the ring ortho/para positions being the electrophile-favoring sites.

Note on precision/regime: these finite-difference Fukui values are computed from Mulliken populations, which are basis-set dependent; treat them as relative reactivity trends rather than transferable absolute numbers. The unusually large magnitudes here reflect the Mulliken charges of the N+1 (anion) state at this basis (the anion charges are large), so ranking the sites is more meaningful than the raw magnitudes.

**Integrity / convergence:** engine reported `integrity.status = ok`, `trustworthy = true`. All three integrity checks passed: fukui_charge_conservation_f+ — "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"; fukui_charge_conservation_f- — "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"; schema_header — "common header present". The result JSON contained **no `warnings` array (no warnings reported)**.

**Software:** PySCF (RKS/UKS, DFT backend). Partial-charge scheme: Mulliken (PySCF).

**Reproducibility.** Command (as run by the engine): `cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`. Result JSON written to `/home/erobles/aniline_fukui_dft.json`; per-atom f+/f−/dual bar chart at `.../20260704-174933_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. A live `fukui_<timestamp>.out` log is streamed to the run's working directory for real-time monitoring (`tail -f`) and post-mortem inspection.
